RAVI MEHTA
CHMRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
August 13, 1997
Robert E. Leidigh
Olson, Hagel, Fong, Leidigh,
Waters & Fishburn
Plaza Towers
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 1425
Sacramento, California 95814
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. 1-97-399
Dear Mr. Leidigh:
This letter is a response to your request br ath ice on belialt’oi your clients Caiiibmia
Newspaper Publishers Association and its lbr-prolit. wholly owned subsidiary, CNPA Services.

FACTS
The California Newspaper Publishers Association and its subsidiary, CNPA Services,
Inc., registered jointly as a lobbyist employer pursuant to advice provided to you in
I )92.
I.culk’/i Advice Letter No. l—)2—hS5. The two entities were rei.istered :us a lobl,visu. employer
because (‘N P.\ Services. Inc.. employed two in—house lobbyists.
In addit ion, the joint entity
employed a separate lobbying lion to assist in its lobbying efforts.
In our iclephonc conversation
ot April 4.

the two lobbyists working bbr it and have California Newspaper Publishers Association as one of

MENTo, CALWORNIA 95814
9i6) 322-5660


A
-
-
File No. 1-97-399
Page 2
QUESTION
What registration forms should be filed on behalf of California Newspaper Publishers
Association and its subsidiary CNPA Services, Inc., to effectuate the changeover in
configurations?
CONCLUSION
In order to terminate their status as a registered lobbyist employer, California Newspaper
Publishers Association and its subsidiary CNPA Services, Inc. must file a Form 606, Notice of
Termination.
Because CNPA Services, Inc., will be receiving payments for lobbying services, it must
file a Form 601, Lobbying Firm Registration Statement.
California Newspaper Publishers Association and the seven newspaper publishers will
each have to file Form 602, Lohhyim Firm Activity Authorization. authorizing CNPA Services,
Inc., to lobby on thcir behalf.
The two in-house lobbyists employed by Calilbrnia Newspaper Publishers Association
and its subsidiary CNPi\ Services, Inc., must each amend thcir Form 604, Lobbyist Certification
Statement when their employment begins with the lobbying firm, CNPA Services, Inc.
Because CNPA Services, Inc., will be authorizing another lobbying firm to lobby on
behalf of several of its clients, Form 602 must be completed by CNPA Services, Inc., and sent to
the other lobbying firm for filing with the Secretary of State’s Office
Sccuons So I 00—So I 07.)
you have any other questions reuarding this matter, please contact me n (01 ( 322—
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel //
L
By:
Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

Association and its subsidiary CNPA Services, Inc., to effectuate the changeover in

CONCLUSION: In order to terminate their status as a registered lobbyist employer, California Newspaper
